states_list.name: INIT
states_list.description: Initial connection state before any command

states_list.name: DATA_CHANNEL_READY
states_list.description: Data connection parameters negotiated; waiting for a transfer command

states_list.name: RENAME_PENDING
states_list.description: Rename source accepted, waiting for RNTO

invariants_list.rule: The PASS command must be sent immediately following a USER command.
Authentication (USER followed by PASS) must be completed before issuing LIST, RETR, or STOR commands.
A PASV or PORT command must be issued prior to any command that initiates a data transfer (LIST, RETR, STOR, NLST, APPE).
If the server responds with a 530 error to a PASS command, the client should restart the authentication sequence with a USER command.
The QUIT command can be issued at any time to terminate the session.
The RNTO command must be preceded by a RNFR command in the same session.
The session begins with a server greeting (Response Code 220) before any client commands are accepted.
USER.username: Must be a valid username string.
PASS.password: Must be a valid password string.
CWD.path: Must be a valid directory path string.
RETR.filename: Must be a valid filename string.
STOR.filename: Must be a valid filename string.
PORT.address_port: Must be formatted as h1,h2,h3,h4,p1,p2.
TYPE.type_code: Must be 'A' for ASCII or 'I' for Image/Binary.

recommended_actions: Several observed message types are not yet represented in the state machine; prioritize seeds and probes for: ACCT, CAPI, CDUP, DELE, FEAT, HELP, MKD, MODE
Some inferred states are weakly connected in the current model; add directed seeds for: RESETTING, RENAME_PENDING

suggested_campaign.objective: Increase post-auth and data-channel coverage while validating weaker transitions.

status_changes.description: The PASS command must be sent immediately following a USER command.
status_changes.rationale: Alignment with RFC 959 standards as identified by documentation analysis.

status_changes.description: Authentication (USER followed by PASS) must be completed before issuing LIST, RETR, or STOR commands.
status_changes.rationale: Standard security requirement in FTP-like protocols documented by the spec agent.

status_changes.description: A PASV or PORT command must be issued prior to any command that initiates a data transfer (LIST, RETR, STOR, NLST, APPE).
status_changes.rationale: Requirement for data channel establishment documented by specification agent.

status_changes.description: If the server responds with a 530 error to a PASS command, the client should restart the authentication sequence with a USER command.
status_changes.rationale: Standard error handling behavior documented by specification agent.

status_changes.description: The QUIT command can be issued at any time to terminate the session.
status_changes.rationale: Standard protocol termination behavior documented by specification agent.

status_changes.description: The RNTO command must be preceded by a RNFR command in the same session.
status_changes.rationale: Standard rename sequence documented by specification agent.

status_changes.description: The session begins with a server greeting (Response Code 220) before any client commands are accepted.
status_changes.rationale: Initial state behavior documented by specification agent.
